tossups.question: This author set out to embody Cicero’s concept of honestum in the style employed in a novel whose plot concerns the cashing of a mysterious twenty-pound cheque. A disabled but overly flirtatious woman created by this author is carried about her house on a sofa. Despite the lawyer Abraham Haphazard’s support, another of this author’s characters resigns his post while under fire from the paper The (*) Jupiter. The Low Church Bishop Proudie takes up Dr. Grantly’s vacant seat in a novel by this author in which the Bishop vetoes the reappointment of Septimus Harding as operator of Hiram’s Hospital. This author’s novel The Warden began a six-novel series whose second entry is named for a cathedral town’s Towers. For 10 points, name this author of the Chronicles of Barsetshire series.
tossups.question_sanitized: This author set out to embody Cicero's concept of honestum in the style employed in a novel whose plot concerns the cashing of a mysterious twenty-pound cheque. A disabled but overly flirtatious woman created by this author is carried about her house on a sofa. Despite the lawyer Abraham Haphazard's support, another of this author's characters resigns his post while under fire from the paper The (*) Jupiter. The Low Church Bishop Proudie takes up Dr. Grantly's vacant seat in a novel by this author in which the Bishop vetoes the reappointment of Septimus Harding as operator of Hiram's Hospital. This author's novel The Warden began a six-novel series whose second entry is named for a cathedral town's Towers. For 10 points, name this author of the Chronicles of Barsetshire series.
tossups.answer: Anthony Trollope (the first line describes The Last Chronicle of Barset)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Anthony Trollope (the first line describes The Last Chronicle of Barset)

tossups.question: In a novel by this author, a penniless man nicknamed “Apollo” ironically receives a large raise only after breaking up with his rural fiancée to pursue the wealthy urbanite Alexandrina. In that novel by this author, Lily Dale is courted by Adolphus Crosbie while living in the title dower house with her mother. In this author’s longest novel, Augustus Melmotte devises a speculation scheme based on a railroad in Utah. The surgeon John Bold challenges the charitable income going to a cathedral precentor created by this author named Septimus Harding. A series of six novels set in a county created by this author include The Small House at Allington and The Warden. For 10 points, name this prolific Victorian novelist who wrote The Way We Live Now and the Chronicles of Barsetshire.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a novel by this author, a penniless man nicknamed "Apollo" ironically receives a large raise only after breaking up with his rural fiancee to pursue the wealthy urbanite Alexandrina. In that novel by this author, Lily Dale is courted by Adolphus Crosbie while living in the title dower house with her mother. In this author's longest novel, Augustus Melmotte devises a speculation scheme based on a railroad in Utah. The surgeon John Bold challenges the charitable income going to a cathedral precentor created by this author named Septimus Harding. A series of six novels set in a county created by this author include The Small House at Allington and The Warden. For 10 points, name this prolific Victorian novelist who wrote The Way We Live Now and the Chronicles of Barsetshire.

tossups.question: In a novel by an author with this surname, a widow-turned-con-artist avoids arrest and escapes back to England during a slave revolt. In a novel by a different author with this surname, a gun-toting American who allegedly fought a duel with her ex-husband travels to England to force a promised marriage to a gentleman. An author with this surname wrote the Widow Barnaby Trilogy and targeted tobacco-spitting in the controversial travelogue Domestic Manners of the Americans. Another author with this surname created a sleazy man from Frisco named Hamilton K. Fisker, who comes to England to fund a new (+) railway from Salt Lake City to Veracruz. In a very long satire by an author with this surname, a speculative bubble engineered by Augustus Melmotte crashes the London stock exchange. For 10 points, give this surname of the author who wrote (*) The Way We Live Now, as well as The Chronicles of Barsetshire.
tossups.answer: Trollope [accept Anthony Trollope; accept Frances “Fanny” Milton Trollope; accept Frances Milton, as that is the maiden name of Frances Trollope]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Trollope [accept Anthony Trollope; accept Frances "Fanny" Milton Trollope; accept Frances Milton, as that is the maiden name of Frances Trollope]

tossups.question: In one work by this person, a woman rejects two suitors by working the words “Old Maid” into her new dress: that story by this person is Never, Never, – Never, Never. In a novel by this person, Ferdinand Lopez withdraws his parliamentary candidacy against Arthur Fletcher after being encouraged to stand by the wife of the title figure. In another novel by this man, Mr. Slope falls from grace with Bishop Proudie’s wife and is turned over in favour of Mr. Arabin for the office of Dean of Barchester. For ten points, The Palliser Novels and The Chronicles of Barsetshire were written by which prolific English novelist of the Victorian era.
tossups.question_sanitized: In one work by this person, a woman rejects two suitors by working the words "Old Maid" into her new dress: that story by this person is Never, Never, - Never, Never. In a novel by this person, Ferdinand Lopez withdraws his parliamentary candidacy against Arthur Fletcher after being encouraged to stand by the wife of the title figure. In another novel by this man, Mr. Slope falls from grace with Bishop Proudie's wife and is turned over in favour of Mr. Arabin for the office of Dean of Barchester. For ten points, The Palliser Novels and The Chronicles of Barsetshire were written by which prolific English novelist of the Victorian era.

tossups.question: A character created by this author plays an imaginary violoncello with his right hand when he becomes angry. In a novel by this man, a caricature of the Times called The Jupiter launches a smear campaign under editor Tom Towers. This author created the de Courcy family, a member of which is courted by Adolphus Crosbie in The Small House at Allington. In another of his novels, Mrs. Proudie blocks the reappointment of the director of Hiram's Hospital. That novel of his begins with Archdeacon Grantly not succeeding his father as a bishop. John Bold attacks the 800 pound income of Septimus Harding despite falling in love with Septimus's daughter, Eleanor, in his novel The Warden. For 10 points, name this Victorian novelist who included Barchester Towers in his Barsetshire Chronicles.
tossups.question_sanitized: A character created by this author plays an imaginary violoncello with his right hand when he becomes angry. In a novel by this man, a caricature of the Times called The Jupiter launches a smear campaign under editor Tom Towers. In another of his novels, Mrs. Proudie blocks the reappointment of the director of Hiram's Hospital. That novel of his begins with Archdeacon Grantly not succeeding his father as a bishop. John Bold attacks the 800 pound income of Septimus Harding despite falling in love with Septimus's daughter, Eleanor, in his novel The Warden. For 10 points, name this Victorian novelist who included Barchester Towers in his Barsetshire Chronicles.

bonuses.leadin: Note to moderator: Read the answerline to the first part carefully. The schemes of one of these two ideological movements are undone at a garden party, when Signora Neroni reveals Obadiah Slope to be a two-timing sycophant. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Note to moderator: Read the answerline to the first part carefully. The schemes of one of these two ideological movements are undone at a garden party, when Signora Neroni reveals Obadiah Slope to be a two-timing sycophant. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these two real-life factions that are represented in an 1857 novel by Theophilus Grantly on one side and Mrs. Proudie and her husband on the other.
That war between High Church and Low Church factions occurs in Barchester Towers,the second entry in this Victorian author’s Chronicles of Barsetshire series.

In The Warden, the previous entry in the series, Trollope satirized this contemporary as Dr. Pessimist Anticant. This author wrote a novel about the fictional German scholar Diogenes Teufels·dröckh (“TOY-fulls-druck”), who studies the “philosophy of clothes.”
parts_sanitized: Name these two real-life factions that are represented in an 1857 novel by Theophilus Grantly on one side and Mrs. Proudie and her husband on the other.
That war between High Church and Low Church factions occurs in Barchester Towers,the second entry in this Victorian author's Chronicles of Barsetshire series.

In The Warden, the previous entry in the series, Trollope satirized this contemporary as Dr. Pessimist Anticant. This author wrote a novel about the fictional German scholar Diogenes Teufelsdrockh ("TOY-fulls-druck"), who studies the "philosophy of clothes."
answers: High Church Anglicanism and Low Church Anglicanism [or High Church Anglicanism and Evangelicalism; or High Church Anglicanism and Evangelical Anglicanism; or High Anglicanism and Low Anglicanism; prompt on high and low]
answers_sanitized: High Church Anglicanism and Low Church Anglicanism [or High Church Anglicanism and Evangelicalism; or High Church Anglicanism and Evangelical Anglicanism; or High Anglicanism and Low Anglicanism; prompt on high and low]
Thomas Carlyle (The novel is Sartor Resartus.)

bonuses.leadin: A pair of robberies in this town is blamed on foreigners, causing its residents to suspect that the turban-wearing Signor Brunoni is a French spy. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this town home to the gossip Miss Pole. A spinster in this town named Matty Jenkyns goes bankrupt and sells off her house to Miss Martha and the town carpenter Jem.

Elizabeth Gaskell's episodic novel Cranford grew in popularity thanks to the illustrations of George, a cartoonist with this surname. His granddaughter with this surname set her novel Rebecca in her native Cornwall.

The use of George du Maurier’s illustrations to boost sales of Cranford was inspired by J.E. Millais’ illustrations for this author’s Framley Parsonage, one of six books set in a fictional, highly clerical English county.
answers: du Maurier [or George du Maurier; or Daphne du Maurier]
Anthony Trollope (the series of novels is The Chronicles of Barsetshire.)
answers_sanitized: du Maurier [or George du Maurier; or Daphne du Maurier]
Anthony Trollope (the series of novels is The Chronicles of Barsetshire.)

parts: Name this author of The Wanderer and the sentimental novel Evelina.

bonuses.leadin: This character’s ignorant nature, like how he ‘knew nothing of any... political question’, prompted a flurry of articles in 2016 that compared this character’s ascendancy to that of Donald Trump. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this character. This character ends up poisoning himself by drinking inappropriate chemicals after his clerk Croll leaves him and his fraudulent scheming over a non-existent Utah railway is revealed.
Melmotte is the antihero of The Way We Live Now, the longest novel by this Victorian author. He described the machinations of ecclesiastical politics in his Chronicles of Barsetshire.
Trollope’s other political novels include the six ‘parliamentary novels’, a series usually titled for this character, who becomes Prime Minister after inheriting the dukedom of Omnium.

“A little black thing among the snow” cries “weep! 'weep!” in this poet’s “The Chimney Sweeper” which is collected in his Songs of Innocence and of Experience.

An author with this surname wrote about a boy who escapes from an industrial mill in the novel Michael Armstrong, the Factory Boy. Another author with this surname wrote the Chronicles of Barsetshire series.
answers: Elizabeth Barrett Browning [or Elizabeth Barrett Browning or Elizabeth Barrett Moulton-Barrett; prompt on Browning]

bonuses.leadin: Hawthorne described a series of novels set in this county as being "as if some giant had hewn a great lump out of the earth and put it in a glass case." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this county, the setting of a novel in which Bishop Proudie takes an ecclesiastical seat that had been reserved for Archdeacon Grantly.

bonuses.leadin: The characters Doctor Pessimist Anticant and Mr. Popular Sentiment in this novel are supposedly parodies of Thomas Carlyle and Charles Dickens respectively. For 10 points each:
parts: This Bastershire-set novel centres on the conflict between Septimus Harding, holder of the titular office, and the young reformer John Bold. Bold enlists the help of the editor of The Jupiter and forces Harding to resign.

This writer of The Warden wrote forty-seven novels, including the sweeping satire The Way We Live Now. He also worked at the General Post Office for over thirty years before resigning to stand as the Liberal candidate for Beverley in Yorkshire.
In this Trollope novel Lizzie Greystock looks for a new husband while trying to stop Mr. Camperdown from retrieving a family heirloom given to her by her late husband Sir Florian.

“The Crown Diamond” is an alternate version of the short story "The Adventure of the Mazarin Stone," which are both works following this title sleuth who lives at 221B Baker Street.

bonuses.leadin: Critic Kenneth Tynan’s pejorative term for middlebrow mid-20th-century British drama, “the Loamshire play,” refers to a fictional county that this author regularly used as a setting. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author of a “Loamshire novel” about an activist who accidentally kills a constable during a riot that breaks out on the day that his candidate, Harold Transome, loses a parliamentary election.

The chaplain Obadiah Slope delivers an unpopular sermon against chanting in church services near the start of the most popular of the six novels this author set in the fictional county of Barsetshire.
This author agglomerated several southwest English counties into the fictional area of Wessex, where he set novels like Far from the Madding Crowd and Tess of the D’Urbervilles.
answers: George Eliot [or Mary Anne Evans] (The novel is Felix Holt.)
Anthony Trollope (The novel is Barchester Towers.)

bonuses.leadin: Supposedly, John Cowper Powys would vigorously bang his head against these objects in order to expedite their function. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this objects that were first introduced to Great Britain in the 1850s by an author who observed their efficiency during a tour of Europe.

This was the author and postal worker who was responsible for introducing freestanding mailboxes to England. It is therefore unsurprising that many of his novels contain lengthy correspondences and letter-reading scenes, such as when Obadiah Slope writes a letter proposing to Eleanor Bold in Barchester Towers .

bonuses.leadin: In his essays, William Gass has repeatedly praised this novel’s sentence “A cow broke in (tomorrow morning) to my Uncle Toby’s fortifications” as one of his favorite in all of English literature. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this digression-filled novel by Laurence Sterne, in which the title character narrates the circumstances surrounding his birth.
To his father’s regret, Tristram Shandy’s nose is crushed at birth when Dr. Slop uses this newly invented device during the operation.

In one of this author’s novels, it is jokingly suggested that Dr. Slop might be an ancestor of Obadiah Slope. In that novel by him, Mrs. Proudie makes sure Septimus Harding is not re-appointed as warden of Hiram’s Hospital.
answers: The Life and Opinions of Tristram Shandy, Gentleman

parts_sanitized: Name this digression-filled novel by Laurence Sterne, in which the title character narrates the circumstances surrounding his birth.
To his father's regret, Tristram Shandy's nose is crushed at birth when Dr. Slop uses this newly invented device during the operation.

In one of this author's novels, it is jokingly suggested that Dr. Slop might be an ancestor of Obadiah Slope. In that novel by him, Mrs. Proudie makes sure Septimus Harding is not re-appointed as warden of Hiram's Hospital.

bonuses.leadin: This novel centers on Lizzie Greystock, a fortune hunter who pursues Lord Fawn and Lord George Carruthers after the death of her husband Sir Florian. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Lizzie is disgraced after the theft of the title jewels on a necklace.
The Eustace Diamonds is the third in this British author’s six Parliamentary novels about the Palliser family. He also wrote Barchester Towers.

This massive novel by Trollope focuses on the suspicions of Louis Trevelyan, who travels to the Mandarin Islands and marries Sir Marmaduke’s daughter Emily Rowley. Olivier Dimsdale starred in its BBC adaptation.

This massive novel by Trollope focuses on the suspicions of Louis Trevelyan, who travels to the Mandarin Islands and marries Sir Marmaduke's daughter Emily Rowley. Olivier Dimsdale starred in its BBC adaptation.

bonuses.leadin: Tom Towers writes influential pieces for The Jupiter in this novel, whose other characters include the Attorney General, Sir Abraham Haphazard. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this first novel of The Chronicles of Barsetshire which centers on Reverend Harding, who resigns the title position taking care of the old men at Hiram’s Hospital.
This author wrote The Chronicles of Barsetshire, including Framley Parsonage and Barchester Towers. You may know this Victorian novelist for producing a voluminous amount of writings.
In a different novel by Anthony Trollope, the incredulously named character of Elias Gotobed runs for office in this country. It is also the setting for much of Dickens’s Martin Chuzzlewit.
answers: United States of America [accept obvious equivalents. That Trollope novel is The American Senator.]
